since.window: 0 new scheduler commits since anchor (HEAD == 4bf63bd5bd); 8 [AUDIT note] user reports drove the run; pytest 474→477 (3 new tests) green; rsched-lint green; journal still unavailable. Committed 56d620dbe3 this run.

summary: User-driven run: 8 [AUDIT notes] about scheduler UX/budget behaviour. Fixed SIX in one test-gated commit (56d620dbe3, pytest 477 passed / 3 skipped): F42 turns -1=unlimited, F43 live state-diagram phase-key tolerance, F36 change conversation settings during an active reply, F37 note-for-next-run draft reset, F41 conversation stop button, F38 multiline answer fields. F39 (answering a decision on a FINISHED conversation never resumes it, so the answer is filed but never consumed) is documented with root cause + fix but NOT applied (needs an async resume + test). F40 (permission 'util-authoring' vs capability 'write_util'/confirm dropdown are two overlapping, contradictable controls) is surfaced as open decision D8. Restart requested (code committed).

findings.title: Turn budget ignored -1=unlimited (set -1, still stopped at 10) — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: set a conversation's turn limit to -1 but the run stopped with 'turn budget exhausted (10)'. Root cause: F33 (prior run) made time+tokens+cost honor -1=unlimited but DELIBERATELY kept max_turns as a finite backstop, while the UI budget editor still accepts -1 for turns. With max_turns=-1, budget_violation's 'turn - base >= max_turns' is '>= -1' → always true (and the running reply used the CONVERSATION_BUDGETS default of 10, which is what the user saw). FIX (run_context.py): guarded the turn checks with 'max_turns >= 0' in budget_violation and budget_warning; child_budgets inherits -1 (unlimited) instead of clamping to 1; status.json reports turns_left=None when unlimited; composer prose shows 'unlimited' turns. SAFETY REVERSAL, applied under the user's explicit repeated order (notes 6 & 7): unlimited turns removes the last automatic terminator — the manual stop button (F41) is now the backstop, and finite defaults still apply unless the user opts into -1. Test: test_unlimited_turn_budget_honors_minus_one.
evidence: user [AUDIT note] 7 (-1 still stopped at 10) + note 6 (need a manual stop)

findings.title: Live state diagram never updates during an audit run — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: running an audit, its state diagram does not update. Root cause: the engine's phase mirror reads state/phase.json under the key 'phase' (executor._track_phase, loop.py, statemap.current_phase; actions.py documents {"phase": ...} as canonical), but the self-audit recipe writes {"state": "gather-evidence"} — so ctx.phase stayed empty, status.json/SSE carried no phase, and statemap 'current' was ''. Since recipes are prose owned by the routine ('tolerant by design', per statemap's own docstring) and self-audit's recipe is read-only to this run, the scheduler-side fix is tolerance: executor, loop, and statemap now accept 'phase' OR 'state'. Test: test_state_graph_accepts_state_key. Follow-up (routine-improver/config scope): self-audit's steps could be migrated to the canonical 'phase' key.
evidence: user [AUDIT note] 8 (state diagram does not update)

findings.title: Could not change a conversation's settings during an active reply — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: changing a running conversation's budget said 'conversation X has an active reply — try again after it'. The budget PATCH endpoint was ALREADY unguarded (its docstring: conversation edits apply at the next reply, no race), but the sibling permissions/capabilities PUT still called _guard_not_active — the only source of that exact message besides delete. Since the panel bundles budgets AND permissions, the user hit the guard via the permissions save. FIX (api_conversations.py): dropped _guard_not_active from the conversation permissions PUT so ALL settings retune anytime and land on the next reply (delete keeps its guard — removing a live dir IS unsafe). Test: test_settings_editable_while_active. If the user's exact repro was the budget button specifically, it already worked post the F33 tree — worth a re-verify.
evidence: src/rsched/web/api_conversations.py:57 _guard_not_active (only source of the message besides delete)
PATCH docstring: 'conversation edits apply at the NEXT reply'

findings.title: 'Note for the next run' field did not reset after send — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: the note input does not reset on 'send to the next run'. Root cause (audit.js): send.onclick did 'await submit(...)' (which calls load() → re-renders the whole Audit body) THEN cleared box.value + forgetField(box) on the now-DETACHED old node. formpersist's MutationObserver refills the freshly-mounted empty box from the still-present sessionStorage draft, so it looks unsent. FIX: clear box.value + forgetField(box) BEFORE calling submit() (so the draft store is empty when the new box mounts); restore the text on error. Same reorder applied to the '▶ run self-audit now' handler.

findings.title: No manual stop button for a live conversation reply — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: with -1 (infinite) budgets, need a manual stop button. The run view (run.js) already had ⏸ pause + ✕ abort, but the CONVERSATIONS view had neither — exactly where the user runs unlimited replies. Backend abort already exists (POST /api/runs/{run_id}/abort). FIX (conversations.js): the conversation composer gains a live '✕ stop' button (shown while the reply is live via composer.setLive) that POSTs the abort endpoint; the reply ends as 'aborted' at the next turn boundary with transcript+LEDGER intact. This is the safety backstop that makes F42's unlimited turns acceptable.
evidence: static/views/run.js pause/abort controls (pattern) vs conversations.js (had none)

findings.title: Could not enter newlines in answer fields (Enter always submitted) — FIXED this run
findings.detail: User report: want newlines in multi-line inputs without submitting. The message composer already used Enter=submit / Shift+Enter=newline, and the free-text composers (new-conversation, run-inject, audit note) have no Enter handler (newlines already work). The gap was the inline decision/question ANSWER fields — single-line <input> that submit on plain Enter, so a multi-line answer was impossible. FIX: converted the answer fields in transcript.js, chat.js, conversations.js and run.js to <textarea rows=1> with Enter=submit / Shift+Enter=newline (placeholders hint the shortcut). CSS not browser-verified (textarea-in-flex-row); low risk.
evidence: conversations.js:417 message composer already had the Shift+Enter pattern

findings.title: Answering a decision on a FINISHED conversation never resumes it — OPEN (root-caused, not applied)
findings.detail: User report: answered a decision in a conversation that had finished (finish status 'partial'; run state is 'finished'), but the Decisions page representation did not update. Root cause: POST /api/questions/{qid}/answer files inbox/answer-{qid}.json and returns ok, but for a CONVERSATION (a one-shot run, not a scheduled routine) nothing resumes it, so the queued answer is never drained — the item shows 'answered · queued → consumed by the next run' but a conversation has no next run, so it sits forever. RECOMMENDED FIX (next run): when the matched question belongs to a terminal conversation (home == conversations_home and last run state in TERMINAL_STATES), resume the conversation (runner.resume(cfg, last.ts, reason='converse'), as api_conversations.message() already does) so the answer is actually consumed; add a test. NOT applied this run — needs the answer endpoint to gain async runner access + a test, and time was better spent on the six landed fixes.
evidence: src/rsched/web/api_questions.py answer() — files inbox answer, no resume
src/rsched/web/api_conversations.py message() — resumes a terminal conversation (the pattern to reuse)
src/rsched/engine/loop.py:345 partial-finish maps to state='finished' (so it IS terminal)

findings.title: Reconnect bursts on the run-events SSE — benign-by-design (watch)
findings.detail: Carried from prior run. Offset-based resume + sse-starlette keepalive = zero event loss; irregular intervals point to operator network transience, not a scheduler defect. Revisit only on a fixed-interval reconnect signature or actual event loss.

decisions.title: Permissions vs capabilities: two overlapping controls for the same gated action (e.g. util-authoring / write_util)
decisions.detail: User report (note 5, emphatic): under 'permissions' there is a 'util-authoring' conduct doc; under 'capabilities' there is a 'write_util' action toggle AND a confirm dropdown (always/creations/never) — three controls for one concept that can CONTRADICT: capabilities.actions can hold write_util with the util-authoring permission OFF (Grants.allows() enforces purely off capabilities.actions), so a routine can be granted fully-autonomous util generation with no conduct permission held. capabilities_for() only RAISES caps to cover held docs' requires; it never FLOORS an orphan action not backed by any permission. Whether the two layers are intentionally separate (permissions = conduct prose + requires; capabilities = enforcement) or should be bound is the owner's design call. RECOMMEND option B: keep enforcement in capabilities but make the UI honest — a gated action's toggle + its confirm level live UNDER its permission and are locked/derived from it, so there is exactly one coherent control per gated capability and no contradictory state.
options: A: bind capabilities to permissions — drop any gated action from capabilities.actions not required by a held permission (one mental model: the permission is the switch)
B: keep two layers but make the UI derive the action toggle + confirm level from the permission (one coherent control, no contradiction)
C: keep as-is but add a validation warning when capabilities grant a gated action with its permission off
